99	      "body": "Created new term **vesicle membrane tethering activity** (GO:7770062) as a child of [GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/QuickGO/term/GO:0140177).\n\n**Definition:** A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion.\n\n**References:**\n- `PMID:19887069` — Sztul & Lupashin 2009, *FEBS Lett*, \"Role of vesicle tethering factors in the ER-Golgi membrane traffic\" (as requested)\n- `PMID:19575650` — Yu & Hughson 2010, *Annu Rev Cell Dev Biol*, \"Tethering factors as organizers of intracellular vesicular traffic\" — a broad review covering tethering factors across intracellular trafficking pathways, including the plasma-membrane exocyst and CATCHR-family complexes (Munc13 has a CATCHR/MUN-domain fold and is a key synaptic vesicle tethering factor)\n\nA note on the second reference: you asked for an article that \"also covers synaptic vesicle transport and targeting.\" Yu & Hughson 2010 is a general review that includes synaptic-relevant tethers (exocyst, CATCHR/Munc13) within its broader scope. If you'd prefer a more synapse-focused companion reference, I'd suggest `PMID:23164531` (Hallermann & Silver 2013, *Trends Neurosci*, \"Sustaining rapid vesicular release at active zones: potential roles for vesicle tethering\") or `PMID:22794257` (Südhof 2012, *Neuron*, \"The presynaptic active zone\"). Happy to swap or add — just let me know.\n\nSynonyms added:\n- `vesicle membrane tether activity` (EXACT)\n- `vesicle tethering activity` (EXACT)\n\nPR: geneontology/go-ontology#31895\n\n---\n🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**\n- Model: `claude-opus-4-6`\n- Agent harness: claude-code\n- Triggered by: @raymond91125\n- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24372402903)",

   - Definition: "A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion."
   - References: PMID:19887069 and a review article about synaptic vesicle transport
   - Parent term: GO:0140177 with extended definition to include "vesicle"

file.content:
[![Build Status](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/workflows/CI/badge.svg)](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions?query=workflow%3ACI)

# Repository for GO Ontology

This repository is primarily for the developers of the GO and contains the source code for the GO ontology. 

More general documentation about GO can be found on the [GO website](http://geneontology.org/); ontology files can be downloaded from the [GO website/downloads](http://geneontology.org/docs/download-ontology/).

You should __NOT__ use this repository to download production versions of GO.

## Ontology Editors

Instructions for editing the ontology can be found in the [GO wiki/Ontology section](http://wiki.geneontology.org/index.php/Ontology_Development#Editing_the_Ontology). 

[Status pages](https://geneontology.github.io/go-ontology/) — ontology developers can review changes in the repository since the last official ontology release

## Ontology Pipeline Overview

 - GitHub Actions performs tests on every commit or pull request - https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions?query=workflow%3ACI
 - A Jenkins job produces SNAPSHOT releases every second day- https://build.geneontology.org/job/geneontology/job/pipeline/job/snapshot/
    * The S3 bucket for go-data-product-snapshot/ontology/, mapped to http://snapshot.geneontology.org/ontology/, is redirected to from the OBO Library PURL (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/go/snapshot/FOO)
 - A Jenkins job produces monthly production releases (https://build.geneontology.org/job/geneontology/job/pipeline/job/release/)

input.content:
# Changes for Issue #31863: MF vesicle membrane tethering activity

## Summary

Created a new molecular function term **vesicle membrane tethering activity** (GO:7770062) and extended the definition of its parent term **membrane-membrane adaptor activity** (GO:0140177) to explicitly include vesicles.

## Changes Made

### 1. New Term: GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity

```
[Term]
id: GO:7770062
name: vesicle membrane tethering activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion." [PMID:19575650, PMID:19887069]
synonym: "vesicle membrane tether activity" EXACT []
synonym: "vesicle tethering activity" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0140177 ! membrane-membrane adaptor activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31863" xsd:anyURI
created_by: dragon-ai-agent
creation_date: 2026-04-13T23:43:55Z
```

**Rationale**: This term captures the specific function of vesicle tethering factors in intracellular membrane traffic, providing molecular-level annotation for proteins like SNARE-binding exocyst and CATCHR complexes involved in both ER-Golgi vesicular traffic and synaptic vesicle targeting.

**References**:
- PMID:19887069: Sztul & Lupashin (2009), "Role of vesicle tethering factors in the ER-Golgi membrane traffic"
- PMID:19575650: Yu & Hughson (2010), "Tethering factors as organizers of intracellular vesicular traffic" — broad review of tethering factors across multiple pathways including exocyst and CATCHR-family complexes

### 2. Updated Term: GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity

**Original definition:**
"The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex or organelle."

**Updated definition:**
"The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex, vesicle or organelle."

**Rationale**: The addition of "vesicle" explicitly clarifies that this parent term encompasses adaptor activities that bridge vesicles to target membranes, making the relationship between parent and child term (GO:7770062) clearer and more precise.

## Validation

- Term is in correct GO namespace (molecular_function)
- Proper is_a relationship to parent term (GO:0140177)
- Definition mirrors the logical/functional scope of the term
- Synonyms are appropriate and marked EXACT
- Metadata includes term_tracker_item, created_by, and creation_date
- References are valid PMIDs from relevant literature

## Metadata Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue intent and scope analyzed and understood
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Parent term GO:0140177 consulted; appropriate for vesicle tethering
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: Molecular function term structure follows existing patterns
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: is_a relationship correctly specified to parent; no over-specification
- [x] METADATA: created_by and creation_date present for new term; term_tracker_item links to issue
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: Both PMIDs validated and appropriate for definition scope
- [x] ISSUE-ALIGNMENT: Changes directly address the requested new term and parent term extension

tool_use_result.content:
# Changes for Issue #31863: MF vesicle membrane tethering activity

## Summary

Created a new molecular function term **vesicle membrane tethering activity** (GO:7770062) and extended the definition of its parent term **membrane-membrane adaptor activity** (GO:0140177) to explicitly include vesicles.

## Changes Made

### 1. New Term: GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity

```
[Term]
id: GO:7770062
name: vesicle membrane tethering activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion." [PMID:19575650, PMID:19887069]
synonym: "vesicle membrane tether activity" EXACT []
synonym: "vesicle tethering activity" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0140177 ! membrane-membrane adaptor activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31863" xsd:anyURI
created_by: dragon-ai-agent
creation_date: 2026-04-13T23:43:55Z
```

**Rationale**: This term captures the specific function of vesicle tethering factors in intracellular membrane traffic, providing molecular-level annotation for proteins like SNARE-binding exocyst and CATCHR complexes involved in both ER-Golgi vesicular traffic and synaptic vesicle targeting.

**References**:
- PMID:19887069: Sztul & Lupashin (2009), "Role of vesicle tethering factors in the ER-Golgi membrane traffic"
- PMID:19575650: Yu & Hughson (2010), "Tethering factors as organizers of intracellular vesicular traffic" — broad review of tethering factors across multiple pathways including exocyst and CATCHR-family complexes

### 2. Updated Term: GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity

**Original definition:**
"The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex or organelle."

**Updated definition:**
"The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex, vesicle or organelle."

**Rationale**: The addition of "vesicle" explicitly clarifies that this parent term encompasses adaptor activities that bridge vesicles to target membranes, making the relationship between parent and child term (GO:7770062) clearer and more precise.

## Validation

- Term is in correct GO namespace (molecular_function)
- Proper is_a relationship to parent term (GO:0140177)
- Definition mirrors the logical/functional scope of the term
- Synonyms are appropriate and marked EXACT
- Metadata includes term_tracker_item, created_by, and creation_date
- References are valid PMIDs from relevant literature

## Metadata Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue intent and scope analyzed and understood
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Parent term GO:0140177 consulted; appropriate for vesicle tethering
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: Molecular function term structure follows existing patterns
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: is_a relationship correctly specified to parent; no over-specification
- [x] METADATA: created_by and creation_date present for new term; term_tracker_item links to issue
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: Both PMIDs validated and appropriate for definition scope
- [x] ISSUE-ALIGNMENT: Changes directly address the requested new term and parent term extension

Excellent! I've verified that issue #31863 has been successfully completed. Here's what was accomplished:

## Work Summary

**Issue**: Create a new molecular function term "vesicle membrane tethering activity" and extend the definition of its parent term.

### Changes Made:

1. **New Term Created: GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity**
   - Namespace: molecular_function
   - Parent: GO:0140177 (membrane-membrane adaptor activity)
   - Definition: "A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion."
   - References: PMID:19575650 (Yu & Hughson 2010) and PMID:19887069 (Sztul & Lupashin 2009)
   - Synonyms: "vesicle membrane tether activity" and "vesicle tethering activity"

2. **Parent Term Updated: GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity**
   - Definition extended to include "vesicle" alongside protein, protein complex, and organelle
   - Now reads: "The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes...to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex, **vesicle** or organelle."

### Deliverables Created:
- ✅ **ISSUE_COMMENTS.md** - Status update for the GitHub issue
- ✅ **PR_COMMENTS.md** - Detailed description of changes with complete checklist

Both files are ready to be fed back to the GitHub repository as specified.
